segments.text: Tifu, by super gluing in my hand to my face during a very important Zoom meeting.
So this happened yesterday, like so many sterling classic posts in Today I Love, word removed, ed up.
And I'm still cringing over it.
It involves superglue, poor life choices, and anxiety-induced stupidity.
To set the scene, I'm working from home like any other warm-blooded human during the pandemic.
On this particular day, I had an insanely nerve-wrackingly important Zoom meeting with the higher-ups in our company.
These were the people who had the power to make or break my career, and I was presenting some ideas to them that I hoped would secure my spot in the company.
Feeling a bit nervous, I decided to make myself feel better by doing something productive.
There was this figurine I had, kinda a mini-hobby I got into during the pandemic.
I had a couple pieces that needed to be fixed.
Enter the villain of the story, superglue.
I superglue the figurine pieces together, feeling pretty satisfied.
But me forgetting that I'm a certified klutz leads to what I can only describe as a Michael Scott level disaster.
I dumped practically half of the superglue onto my hands in my hurry before the meeting.
No time to clean up properly because the Zoom call notification pinged.
I hesitantly wiped my hands on a cloth, not enough apparently, and I swiped to join the meeting.
Meeting begins, everything is going smoothly, presenting my stuff, and nobody figured out that I was a total mess yet.
Until I make the mistake of scratching an itch on my face.
What I didn't realize was that there was a dollop of super glue still on my hand.
Yes, I glued my hand to my face.
On, A, zoom, meeting.
I started to panic internally, trying to act natural with my hand stuck to my face while the presentation was still going on, hoping that none of the corporate bosses noticed.
I tried to ever so slightly move my hand off my face, but it was glued there, and it hurt to even twitch my fingers.
One brave soul finally asked, Opie, is everything okay?
Why is your hand on your face?
The cat was out of the bag, and my stupidity laid bare in front of my bosses.
I had to explain in excruciating detail while still gripping my face.
story to a group of very amused, slightly horrified co-workers and bosses.
Well, at least they won't forget me.
Ever.
T. O. R. D. R. I glued my hand to my face during the most important Zoom meeting of my career because of anxiety and stupidity.
Lesson learned.
Superglue and Zoom meetings do not mix.
